5 min read
Just now

--

ChatGPT is a powerful tool that can be used effectively by everyone, but here are 10 tips to help you get the most out of it.
On my initial use of ChatGPT, I posted a basic question and received a mediocre response. I can recall myself saying, “Why is everybody talking about this tool?” I realised that it wasn’t ChatGPT that had the issue, but a few days later. The way that I asked my questions. Unlike search engines, where you enter some keywords and hope you find what you are looking for, ChatGPT isn’t that. It’s more along the lines of someone who is trying to help but requires enough context to provide you with a helpful answer. When I altered my approach with it, the answers got a lot better. Note that if you are new to ChatGPT, you may want to try out these 10 tips to get more out of your first chat.
1. Give Clear Instructions
What if you contacted somebody and said, “Help me with my business"? Most people would not have an idea of what to do first. Now look at the other one: I'm starting an eCommerce clothing business, and I have a marketing budget of $500. So, what are some great ideas that I can do to attract my original customers that are not too expensive? The second question provides background information, which helps to make it easier to give a helpful answer. That’s also the case with ChatGPT. The more specific you make your request, the more suitable the reply.
2. Explain Your Goal
Don't ask for something; ask for something. Provide reasons for its use. Instead of saying, 'Write an email', try saying, 'Write a friendly follow-up e-mail to a client who has failed to respond to an e-mail in 2 weeks or more, and keep it under 150 words. You’ll get a more suitable tone and structure from ChatGPT if you include your objective.
3. Include Important Details
Many beginners tend to skip this step. If you’re seeking travel tips and advice, state your budget. When asking for help with studying, state what level of schooling you have. When you’re creating content, talk about your readers. Little things can have a great impact. Encourage ChatGPT to assume various roles. Have ChatGPT assume different roles. The simplest method to enhance responses is to give a role. For example: "Be a seasoned career coach. Or act like a “financial educator" and teach this to a total financial novice. The role provides ChatGPT with greater direction to give its answer.
4. Have ChatGPT play a role.
Tasks can be simplified by giving them a job to do, one of the easiest improvements to respond to. For example, be a seasoned veteran career coach or be a “financial educator" talking to a complete beginner. The role provides ChatGPT with more guidance on how to respond.
5. Divide large assignments into smaller ones.
You have said that you are going to begin a blog. Instead of asking, "Teach me blogging.” Try this approach: so one has to first ask about how to pick a niche. Next ask how to learn to find keywords. Then discuss with the children how to write their first article. Lastly, ask how to promote it. One step at a time makes learning a lot easier.
6. Request Alternative Texts
If the first answer is not exactly correct, do not give up! Ask questions like 'Would this be too long?' May be more simply written. Can you think of 3 examples? Consider ChatGPT as your writing partner, not a machine that can provide a single answer.
7. Verify Important Information
ChatGPT can provide a great explanation of ideas, but it’s not perfect. When it comes to medical or legal information or financial choices or fast-changing news, it is always best to double-check on what you have seen or heard from a trusted source before making a decision. Use ChatGPT as a tool to help, not as a final decision-maker. Don’t use it to complete tasks; use it to learn! Many new users of ChatGPT make a big mistake by asking for all their answers from the AI. The better way to do this is to learn from it. For instance, do not copy code; rather, ask the question of why does the code works. Avoid getting essay answers; ask for explanations that will help you understand the topic. The more you know, the more useful and valuable the tool is.
8. Educate with It, Rather Than Complete Tasks:
Many new users of ChatGPT make the error of expecting it to perform all the work for them. Instead, it’s better to learn from it. For instance, rather than copying code, ask, “Why does this code work? Ask for explanations (as opposed to essay responses) to understand the topic. The more you know, the more useful the tool will be.
9. Continue the discussion.
It’s not necessary to begin again at the start of each year. If ChatGPT provides you with a satisfactory answer, follow-up with it. Ask follow-up questions. Request examples. Request it to break down tough parts. In subsequent interactions, the result gets better and better since ChatGPT “remembers” the context of the conversation.
10. Make up better prompts:
Prompt = instructions. The clearer you are on instructions, the more successful you will be. The following simple format can be used: State your goal. Provide background information. Mention your audience. Specify the format. Describe the tone. For example: Write a beginner’s guide to budgeting for Medium that is in simple English and uses a real-life example, and maintain a conversational tone. The one prompt is more effective than just writing, "Explain budgeting.” A Real-World Example Let’s suppose that there are two university students. The first type: "Explain artificial intelligence.” The second type: I’m a university freshman who doesn’t have a technical background, explain artificial intelligence in non-technical terms with examples using everyday language, not more than 500 words. Both pupils were asked the same question. A general explanation was given for one. The other got an answer that was suitable for him. The difference a good prompt can make is that. One Final Thought Don’t have to learn everything at once! As with any skill, learning to use ChatGPT effectively is a practice that will benefit from use. Begin with more precise questions first. Add context. Continue to make your prompts more specific. You’ll discover that ChatGPT isn’t just a search engine; it’s a trusted partner that can assist you in your work, learning, and creativity in no time. What is something new you’ve been using ChatGPT for recently? I’d love to hear how it’s helping you – or if you got stuck.
